question: In the BPMN XML of a Camunda 8.8 multi-instance service task, which attribute distinguishes a sequential multi-instance from a parallel one?

a.text: The XML example for a sequential multi-instance service task uses `<bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics isSequential="true">`. Parallel is the default and omits this attribute.

b.text: There is no `isParallel` attribute on `<zeebe:loopCharacteristics>`. That element holds `inputCollection`, `inputElement`, `outputCollection`, and `outputElement` only.

c.text: No `executionMode` attribute is defined on `<bpmn:serviceTask>` for this purpose. The sequential/parallel distinction lives on the loop characteristics element.

d.text: No `order` attribute exists on `<zeebe:loopCharacteristics>`. The standard BPMN flag for sequential execution is `isSequential` on `<bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics>`.

explanation: A multi-instance activity is parallel by default. To declare it sequential, the BPMN XML sets `isSequential="true"` on `<bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics>`. The Camunda-specific `<zeebe:loopCharacteristics>` extension carries `inputCollection`, `inputElement`, `outputCollection`, and `outputElement`, not the sequential/parallel flag.

codeBlocks.stem:
<bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics isSequential="true">
  <bpmn:extensionElements>
    <zeebe:loopCharacteristics
        inputCollection="= items" inputElement="item"
        outputCollection="results" outputElement="= result" />
  </bpmn:extensionElements>
</bpmn:multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics>
